content: Mechanism: any game with a player avatar visible on a canvas. Lowest-precision recipe; matches as a backup when a more specific mechanism recipe also matches (multi-recipe selection).

checklist: Is a player character or vehicle visible on the canvas?
Is the player inside the canvas (not cut off at the edges)?
Is the canvas non-blank (something is drawn)?
Is a score or HUD visible?
recipe.fix_hint: Player not visible / clipped at edges / not on canvas: usually a state initialization issue. Check player.x and player.y constants — clamp to canvas bounds at init (player.x = Math.max(20, Math.min(canvas.width-20, player.x))). If no player drawn at all, the fix is usually one missing drawImage call in draw(), not a render-loop rewrite.

content: Mechanism: tile-based grid where the player navigates corridors and cannot pass through walls. Matches maze-style games regardless of theme.

checklist: Is a maze or grid playfield visible?
Is the player character (or snake head) visible?
Are blocked/edge regions OR the playfield background visually distinct from the player/collectibles (maze: wall≠corridor; open-grid snake: dark field≠snake/food)?
Are collectibles (food/dots/items) or an exit marker visible?

auto_probes.name: auto_player_not_in_wall
auto_probes.expr: (()=>{const s=window.state||window.gameState;if(!s)return true;const p=s.player||s.pacman||s.hero||s.avatar;if(!p||typeof p.x!=='number'||typeof p.y!=='number')return true;const m=s.maze||s.grid||s.map||s.tiles;if(!Array.isArray(m)||!Array.isArray(m[0]))return true;const tx=Math.floor(p.x);const ty=Math.floor(p.y);if(!m[ty]||m[ty][tx]===undefined)return true;const cell=m[ty][tx];return cell!==1&&cell!=='#'&&cell!=='W'&&cell!=='wall';})()

auto_probes.name: auto_maze_has_walls
auto_probes.expr: (()=>{const s=window.state||window.gameState;if(!s)return true;const m=s.maze||s.grid||s.map||s.tiles;if(!Array.isArray(m)||!Array.isArray(m[0]))return true;const rows=m.length,cols=m[0].length;if(rows<3||cols<3)return false;let walls=0,open=0;for(let r=0;r<rows;r++){if(!Array.isArray(m[r])||m[r].length!==cols)return false;for(let c=0;c<cols;c++){const v=m[r][c];if(v===1||v==='#'||v==='W'||v==='wall')walls++;else open++;}}return walls>0&&open>0&&walls/(walls+open)>0.12;})()

auto_probes.name: auto_chaser_moves_autonomously
auto_probes.expr: (()=>{return (async()=>{const s=window.state||window.gameState;if(!s)return true;const arr=s.chasers||s.enemies||s.pursuers||s.ghosts;if(!Array.isArray(arr)||!arr.length)return true;const c=arr[0];if(!c)return true;const snap=(e)=>typeof e.tx==='number'?e.tx+','+e.ty:typeof e.x==='number'?Math.round(e.x)+','+Math.round(e.y):null;const p0=snap(c);if(!p0)return true;await new Promise(r=>setTimeout(r,900));const p1=snap(arr[0]);return p0!==p1;})()})()

auto_probes.name: auto_vulnerability_mechanism_exposed
auto_probes.expr: (()=>{const s=window.state||window.gameState;if(!s)return true;const hasTimer=typeof s.vulnerableTimer==='number'||typeof s.powerTimer==='number'||typeof s.scaredTimer==='number';if(hasTimer)return true;const arr=s.chasers||s.pursuers||s.ghosts;if(!Array.isArray(arr)||!arr.length){if(Array.isArray(s.enemies)&&s.enemies.length)return true;return true;}return arr.some(e=>e&&typeof e.mode==='string'&&/vulner|fright|flee|scared/i.test(e.mode));})()

auto_probes.name: auto_collectibles_counter
auto_probes.expr: (()=>{const s=window.state||window.gameState;if(!s)return true;const keys=['collectiblesLeft','dotsLeft','dotsRemaining','pelletsLeft','pelletCount','remainingDots'];for(const k of keys){if(k in s&&typeof s[k]!=='number')return false;}return true;})()

auto_probes.name: auto_solid_tile_blocks_move
auto_probes.expr: (()=>{const s=window.state||window.gameState;if(!s)return true;const m=s.maze||s.grid||s.map||s.tiles||s.earth;const rockVals=new Set([2,3,4,'rock','R','solid','earth','dirt','stone']);let hasRock=false;if(Array.isArray(m)&&Array.isArray(m[0])){outer:for(let r=0;r<m.length;r++){for(let c=0;c<m[r].length;c++){if(rockVals.has(m[r][c])){hasRock=true;break outer;}}}}const digCtx=hasRock||Array.isArray(s.rocks)||!!s.digger||!!s.tunnel||!!s.carving;if(!digCtx)return true;const fn=window.game&&(window.game.canMoveTo||window.game.canMove||window.game.isWalkable||window.game.isSolid);if(typeof fn!=='function'||!Array.isArray(m)||!Array.isArray(m[0]))return true;for(let r=0;r<m.length;r++){for(let c=0;c<m[r].length;c++){if(rockVals.has(m[r][c])){try{const ok=(fn.length>=2)?(fn(c,r)||fn(r,c)):fn(m[r][c]);if(ok===true)return false;}catch(e){}return true;}}}return true;})()

recipe.fix_hint: If maze walls invisible: fix maze builder data, not the renderer. If open-grid snake and contrast fails: do NOT invent a maze — recolor playfield vs snake/food sprites. Collision stays one if-check before applying movement. If items missing, fix spawn — not draw().

content: Mechanism: two characters at different x positions, oriented toward each other. Covers fighters, duels, and side-by-side multiplayer.

checklist: Are two distinct characters visible on screen?
Is one character on the left and one on the right?
Are health bars or life counters visible?
Are both characters fully inside the canvas?

auto_probes.name: auto_actors_face_each_other_strict
auto_probes.expr: (()=>{const s=window.state||window.gameState;if(!s)return true;const players=(Array.isArray(s.players)&&s.players)||(Array.isArray(s.fighters)&&s.fighters)||[s.p1,s.p2,s.player1,s.player2,s.fighter1,s.fighter2].filter(Boolean);if(players.length<2)return true;const sorted=players.slice(0,2).sort((x,y)=>(x.x||0)-(y.x||0));const left=sorted[0],right=sorted[1];const fl=(left.facing!==undefined?left.facing:left.direction);const fr=(right.facing!==undefined?right.facing:right.direction);if(typeof fl!=='number'||typeof fr!=='number')return true;return fl>0&&fr<0;})()

recipe.fix_hint: If facing-direction is wrong, wrap the affected character's drawImage in ctx.save(); ctx.translate(x+w/2, y+h/2); ctx.scale(-1, 1); ctx.drawImage(sprite, -w/2, -h/2, w, h); ctx.restore(). Do NOT restructure the facing-direction state machine or touch other characters' draw code. If actors are clustered on one side, just change a single position constant (x = canvas.width - 100). If the attack isn't visible, the fix is usually in the attack-spawn function only — do not refactor draw(). If the attack pose is not visible or the character's look changes between frames, see playbook bullets `attack-must-read-visually` and `animation-frames-consistent-character`: generate each pose frame as TXT2IMG with the SAME detailed character description (the fixed seed keeps the character consistent while the pose changes) — do NOT use low-strength img2img/from_image (it returns the idle pose) and never draw the limb in code.

fix_hints.1: Two fighters not visible: check draw loop and spawn positions.
fix_hints.2: Both on same side: spread spawn x apart (e.g. 25% and 75% width).
fix_hints.3: Both fighters must drawImage their idle/punch/kick frames — ASSETS_LOADED_BUT_UNDRAWN on fighter sprites blocks shipping.
fix_hints.4: Clipped at edges: increase margin or reduce sprite scale.

content: Mechanism: character moves on/near platforms with gravity-consistent pose. Side-view, multiple heights, scrolling background optional.

checklist: Is the player character visible?
Are platforms or ground visible?
Is the player on or near a platform?
Is a score or lives HUD visible?

auto_probes.name: auto_platformer_player_has_vertical_motion_state
auto_probes.expr: (()=>{const s=window.state||window.gameState;if(!s)return true;const p=s.player||s.hero||s.avatar;if(!p)return true;const hasVy=typeof p.vy==='number'||typeof p.dy==='number'||(p.vel&&typeof p.vel.y==='number');return hasVy&&typeof p.y==='number';})()

recipe.fix_hint: Gravity wrong: one constant in the update loop (player.vy += 0.5; player.y += player.vy). Player falling through floor: one if-check in collision (if (player.y + player.h > platform.y) { player.y = platform.y - player.h; player.vy = 0; }). Add ONLY the missing check; do NOT restructure existing platforms array or rewrite physics.

fix_hints.4: Wire drawImage for every generated sprite key in player/enemy draw paths — ASSETS_LOADED_BUT_UNDRAWN means PNG exists but steady-state draw still uses fillRect. See draw-generated-sprites-not-boxes.
fix_hints.5: Player walk/jump/hang poses must cycle drawImage frames — not one static box. See animation-frames-consistent-character.

content: Mechanism: 3D perspective view with weapon overlay and crosshair. Enemies as billboard 2D sprites in the 3D scene.

checklist: Does the view show 3D depth (walls or floor with perspective)?
Is a crosshair or center marker visible?
Are walls and floor visually distinct?
Is a health or ammo HUD visible?

auto_probes.name: auto_fp_map_has_structure
auto_probes.expr: (()=>{const s=window.state||window.gameState;if(!s)return true;const m=s.map||s.maze||s.level||s.walls||s.sectors;if(m===undefined||m===null)return true;if(Array.isArray(m)){if(m.length===0)return false;if(Array.isArray(m[0]))return m.some(row=>Array.isArray(row)&&row.length>0);return m.length>0;}if(typeof m==='object')return Object.keys(m).length>0;return true;})()

recipe.fix_hint: Camera not turning: yaw isn't being updated — check the mousemove handler (player.yaw += e.movementX * 0.003). Forward direction inverted: flip the sign of Math.sin/Math.cos in the move vector, NOT the input handler. Wall textures missing: the texture-lookup function returned the wrong asset key; fix the lookup, not the raycaster.

content: Mechanism: top-down view; player centered or near center; multiple enemies; visible projectiles when firing. Asteroids / Robotron family.

checklist: Is the player ship or avatar visible?
Are enemy objects visible on screen?
Is the playfield non-blank?
Is a score or HUD visible?

auto_probes.name: auto_topdown_player_can_face_directions
auto_probes.expr: (()=>{const s=window.state||window.gameState;if(!s)return true;const p=s.player||s.hero||s.avatar;if(!p)return true;const hasFacing=p.facing!==undefined;const hasAngle=p.angle!==undefined;const hasDir=p.direction!==undefined;if(!hasFacing&&!hasAngle&&!hasDir)return true;const f=hasFacing?p.facing:(hasAngle?p.angle:p.direction);return typeof f==='number';})()

auto_probes.name: auto_enemies_spawn_ahead_of_player
auto_probes.expr: (()=>{const s=window.state||window.gameState||window.game;if(!s)return true;const p=s.player||s.ship||s.hero;if(!p||typeof p.y!=='number')return true;const es=s.enemies||s.aliens||s.invaders||s.rocks||[];if(!Array.isArray(es)||!es.length)return true;const ahead=es.filter(e=>e&&typeof e.y==='number'&&e.y<p.y-8);return ahead.length>0;})()

recipe.fix_hint: Top-down player not visible / wrong direction: state.player must expose facing OR angle. Movement bug: arrow keys set vx/vy; normalize diagonals (vx/=Math.sqrt(2)). Projectile invisible: spawn at player.x,player.y with velocity matching facing — the bug is usually a missing push to state.projectiles[], not a render-loop issue.

fix_hints.1: Player not visible: check spawn position and drawImage call.
fix_hints.2: No enemies: verify wave spawn loop runs each frame.
fix_hints.3: Blank playfield: ensure background and entities draw before HUD.
fix_hints.4: Missing HUD: draw score text in a fixed corner each frame.

content: Mechanism: turn-based ≥3×3 grid board with distinct piece shapes and two colors. Chess, checkers, Tic-Tac-Toe, Go.

checklist: Is a board grid visible (at least 3 by 3)?
Are game pieces visible on the board?
Does the board fill most of the canvas?
Is a turn indicator or score visible?
recipe.fix_hint: Selection not visible: add ONE if-block in draw() that strokes a highlight around state.selected (ctx.strokeStyle='yellow'; ctx.strokeRect(col*S, row*S, S, S)). Wrong piece moved: bug is in the click handler's index math — fix the (col, row) → board[row][col] mapping, not the move-validation function. Turn not advancing: state.currentPlayer = (state.currentPlayer + 1) % 2 after the legal move, before the next render.

content: Mechanism: playfield grid with falling/swappable pieces; preview / next-piece / queue area. Tetris, Bejeweled, Columns family.

checklist: Is a rectangular playfield grid visible?
Are blocks or pieces visible with distinct colors?
Is a score or level indicator visible?
Are pieces aligned to grid cells?
recipe.fix_hint: Pieces stuck / lines don't clear: the clear/merge function isn't being called. Add ONE call to clearLines() or checkMerges() inside the input handler AFTER the move applies. Score not updating: state.score isn't being incremented inside the clear function — do not refactor the renderer; the bug is one missing assignment.

content: Mechanism: track with depth perspective; player vehicle lower-middle; obstacles / opponents ahead. Pole Position / Out Run family.

checklist: Is a road or track visible with perspective?
Is the player vehicle visible in the lower half?
Is a speed or lap HUD visible?
Is the road distinct from the scenery?
recipe.fix_hint: Road not visible: state.road must be an array of segments — the bug is the build function. Speed not advancing: ArrowUp keydown should add to state.player.speed, with friction subtracting per frame. Steering inverted: flip the sign of player.x +/- steer*dt, NOT the road-curvature math.

content: Mechanism: many small distinct elements with frame-to-frame variation — particle systems, fluid sims, fireworks demos.

checklist: Are many small elements visible on the canvas?
Do the elements vary in color or size?
Are elements spread across the canvas?
Is the background distinct from the particles?
recipe.fix_hint: Particles missing / nothing visible: emitter not spawning — the bug is one missing state.particles.push({x,y,vx,vy,life,color}) call. Particle count exploding: cap state.particles.length to a max (e.g. 800); drop oldest when over. Wrong blend: ctx.globalCompositeOperation='lighter' for fire/lasers, default for smoke/blood.

content: Mechanism: paddle on one axis + bouncing ball + breakable targets. Covers Breakout, Arkanoid, Pong-clones, brick-smasher variants, Plinko-style deflection games.

checklist: Is a paddle visible at an edge of the canvas?
Is a ball visible on the canvas?
Are bricks/blocks visible (breakout) OR a second opposing paddle / center net (pong)?
Is a score or lives HUD visible?
recipe.fix_hint: Ball stuck / out of bounds: fix bounce bounds (if (ball.x < 0 || ball.x > canvas.width) ball.vx *= -1). Paddle not moving: keydown must read keys[code] (not e.key). Pong with no bricks: do NOT invent a brick field — ensure the opposing paddle and/or center net are drawn. Breakout missing bricks: fix the brick-array spawn, not draw().

content: Mechanism: horizontal (or vertical) lanes with moving hazards; player hops between lanes / safe zones to reach a goal area. Covers Frogger, Crossy Road, river-crossing games, highway-dodgers.

checklist: Are parallel lanes visible?
Is the player character visible?
Are moving hazards visible in the lanes?
Is a score or lives HUD visible?
recipe.fix_hint: Obstacles missing: each lane needs an obstacles[] populated at init. Player can't advance: ArrowUp handler must decrement state.player.row (or increment depending on direction). No collision: AABB-check player vs each obstacle EVERY frame; the bug is usually the check missing from update(), not the bounding boxes themselves.

content: Mechanism: scene/room backdrop + visible inventory + click-to-interact cursor + dialog text. Covers Maniac Mansion, Monkey Island, King's Quest, Day of the Tentacle and most LucasArts/Sierra adventures.

checklist: Is a room or scene backdrop visible?
Are characters or items visible in the scene?
Is an inventory or item list visible?
Is a dialog or text area visible?
Are dashed or colored debug boxes (if visible) centered on obvious interactive objects (NPCs, items, doors)?
Does each labeled hotspot debug rectangle (if visible) sit over a matching visible sprite or object — not empty background?
Does the cursor change to a pointer or hand when hovering an interactive object?
When clicking an obvious object (character or item), does dialog or inventory visibly change?
recipe.fix_hint: Hotspot misses: use EMPTY bg_* + draw each hotspot object as its own sprite AT the hotspot rect (drawImage(sprite(h.art), h.x, h.y, h.w, h.h)); hit-test the same rect via getBoundingClientRect. Cursor: pointer on hover. See playbook pointclick-hotspot-from-source-art + spatial-interaction-alignment-general.

fix_hints.5: Hotspot debug boxes must cover the visible sprite/object, not empty floor/wall. Each hotspot needs art:'<sprite_key>' drawn at {x,y,w,h}; do NOT bake interactive objects into bg_*.
fix_hints.6: Add mousemove hit-test + canvas.style.cursor='pointer' over hotspots.
fix_hints.7: Click handler must fire on_click for visible NPC/item; verify getBoundingClientRect mapping.
fix_hints.3: Iter-1 draw: every generated bg_* and player_* key MUST use drawImage/sprite(key) — ASSETS_LOADED_BUT_UNDRAWN means PNGs exist but steady-state draw still uses fillRect/pink MISSING.
fix_hints.4: Expose state.scenes (or window.SCENES): each scene needs { bg, hotspots[{x,y,w,h,on_click}] } so pointclick-puzzle-chain and inventory puzzle chain can verify.
fix_hints.1: Room backdrop missing or pink: draw active scene bg via drawImage(scenes[scene].bg) every frame — ASSETS_LOADED_BUT_UNDRAWN means PNG exists but draw still uses fillRect.
fix_hints.2: Characters/items show pink MISSING: wire drawImage for every player_* / npc_* / item_* key in the scene draw loop (see draw-generated-sprites-not-boxes).

content: Mechanism: isometric (rhombus / diamond) tile grid for placement / city-builder / RTS / Diablo-like iso layers. Covers Marble Madness (tilt), puzzle tilt games, and flat iso tile maps — NOT pyramid hoppers (see canvas-pyramid-hopper).

checklist: Are tiles drawn in isometric diamond shape?
Is the player visible on a tile?
Are tile colors visually distinct?
Is a score or progress HUD visible?
recipe.fix_hint: Tiles overlapping / depth wrong: render row-by-row from top (small y first). Click doesn't select right tile: invert the iso projection precisely — col=(sx/(TILE_W/2)+sy/(TILE_H/2))/2; row=(sy/(TILE_H/2)-sx/(TILE_W/2))/2. Do NOT refactor the projection formula; off-by-one or sign issues are the typical bugs.

content: Mechanism: triangular pyramid of stacked isometric cubes; hero hops diagonally between cubes and recolors them. Covers Q*bert and similar pyramid-hop arcade games.

checklist: Is a pyramid of stacked cubes visible (not a flat diamond grid)?
Is the hero visible on a cube?
Do cubes change color when landed on?
Is a score or lives HUD visible?
recipe.fix_hint: Flat diamond grid instead of stacked cubes: use triangular row i with j=0..i and draw cube faces with vertical extrusion. Wrong hop directions: use hop_ur=(i-1,j), hop_ul=(i-1,j-1), hop_dl=(i+1,j), hop_dr=(i+1,j+1) exactly.

content: Mechanism: tiled overworld with camera following player; distinct terrain tiles; NPCs / towns / dungeons visible at map locations. Covers Ultima series, Zelda overworld, Final Fantasy field maps, Dragon Quest world.

checklist: Is a tiled overworld map visible?
Is the player character visible on the map?
Are terrain types visually distinct?
Is a health or inventory HUD visible?

auto_probes.name: auto_overworld_player_has_tile_coords
auto_probes.expr: (()=>{const s=window.state||window.gameState;if(!s)return true;const p=s.player||s.hero||s.avatar;if(!p)return true;const tx=p.tx!==undefined?p.tx:p.col;const ty=p.ty!==undefined?p.ty:p.row;return typeof tx==='number'&&typeof ty==='number';})()

recipe.fix_hint: Player walking through walls: tile-collision check missing — before applying move, check map[ty][tx] !== WALL. NPC dialogue not opening: the keydown handler must check (player.tx, player.ty) is adjacent to an NPC; set state.dialogue.open=true and state.dialogue.npc=npc. Don't refactor the scene switch; the bug is usually the adjacency check or the open-flag assignment.

content: Mechanism: grid-based map with multiple zone types + roads + buildings + economy HUD. Covers SimCity, RollerCoaster Tycoon, Theme Park, Cities Skylines (lite shape), and most tycoon / management games.

checklist: Is a grid map with buildings or zones visible?
Are roads or paths visible?
Is a tool palette or placement UI visible?
Are money or population HUD readouts visible?

auto_probes.name: auto_city_has_tiles_and_resources
auto_probes.expr: (()=>{const s=window.state||window.gameState;if(!s)return true;const t=s.tiles||s.map||s.grid;const r=s.resources||s.economy;if(!Array.isArray(t)||!Array.isArray(t[0]))return true;if(!r||typeof r!=='object')return true;return t.length>=4;})()

recipe.fix_hint: Resources not ticking: per-tick loop missing — add setInterval OR an inside-RAF accumulator that iterates state.buildings and updates state.resources. Can't place a building: hit-test in mousedown handler is broken; verify (clientX-rect.left) is mapped to tile coords. Do NOT refactor the building-generation logic; the bug is the input mapping or the tick scheduler.

content: Mechanism: ship in space + market/inventory UI + combat + navigation. Covers Elite, Star Control, EVE Online (lite shape), Privateer, X-Wing variants with trading.

checklist: Is the player ship visible?
Are stars or planets visible in the background?
Are enemy ships or stations visible when present?
Is a credits or fuel HUD visible?
recipe.fix_hint: Can't navigate: the click/select handler must set state.player.target=systemId; the travel function reads target. Fuel not consuming: travel function missing 'state.player.fuel -= distance' line — add it before the position update. Market not trading: buy/sell button handlers must mutate state.player.cargo AND state.player.money atomically — the bug is usually a missing money-deduct or a stale price snapshot.

content: Mechanism: vertical level with multiple platform floors connected by ladders, hazards that cascade from top to bottom, player progresses upward to a goal. Covers Donkey Kong, BurgerTime, Lode Runner-lite, Crazy Climber, climbing puzzles. Distinct from canvas-side-scroll-platformer (horizontal Mario / Sonic family) because the level GOAL is vertical and ladders are first-class entities.

checklist: Are multiple horizontal floors visible at different heights?
Are ladders visible connecting floors?
Is the player near the bottom of the level?
Is a score or lives HUD visible?
recipe.fix_hint: Ladders floating / buried: derive ladder.yTop and yBot from platform y-values at INIT time (not hardcoded pixels). Falling hazards stop appearing: the spawner needs a respawn loop — splice off-screen hazards and add fresh ones at the top. Player climbing through floor: the climb check needs `if (player.onLadder && keyDown)` else the gravity still applies.

fix_hints.4: Monster/floor/helicopter sprites must use drawImage — undrawn PNGs gate the build. Wire ASSETS[key] into every entity draw path.

content: Generic fallback when no mechanism recipe matches. Asks the VLM for a brief open-ended critique.

checklist: Is the canvas non-blank?
Are main game objects visible?
Is a HUD or score visible?
Are objects distinct from the background?
recipe.fix_hint: Canvas blank / nothing rendering: requestAnimationFrame loop not started — add a single requestAnimationFrame(frame) call at init AFTER assets load. State not exposed: add 'window.gameState = window.state = state' at the end of init so probes can read state. These two lines fix 80% of 'nothing happens on screen' reports.

content: Mechanism: ONE character showcased on a single stage with triggered move animations and NO opponent. Covers single-character animation tests / character showcases / move-set demos.

checklist: Is exactly one character visible on the canvas?
Is the character drawn large (not tiny in a corner)?
Is a move name or frame label HUD visible?
Is the canvas non-blank?
recipe.fix_hint: If a move looks like idle, that pose frame didn't render a distinct pose — regenerate it as TXT2IMG with the SAME detailed character description + the named pose ('right arm fully extended forward, fist out'); img2img at guidance_scale=0 can't change a pose. If the character is tiny, raise its draw size to ~0.4*canvas height. NEVER draw the limb in code over the sprite. A static idle that reads as 'frozen' just needs a subtle idle bob (see ambient-idle-pixel-delta).

content: Mechanism: scripted cinematic scenes built from cycled still frames, with a large centered timed prompt the player must answer with the correct key inside a window (quick-time event). Covers Dragon's Lair / Space Ace and reaction-cutscene games. Motion is intentionally still-frame (laserdisc cadence), NOT smooth — do not judge smoothness. The threat should still visibly approach or wind up so the reaction timing is readable from the picture, not only the prompt word. Dragon's Lair-style goals use a default 8-scene template when the goal is silent; otherwise match the user's scene count and art direction. Each hazard must visibly approach the threatened body part so the correct action is readable from the picture.

checklist: Is the scene rendered from generated image art (full backdrop or generated still), not mostly blank boxes or procedural rectangles?
If generated media exists, are pink/MISSING boxes absent from the visible scene?
Is a large, centered prompt word visible during the reaction window?
Is a lives or scene-number HUD visible?
Is the hazard or threat visibly closer to the hero at the reaction moment than at scene start (not static until keypress)?
Does the hazard move toward the body part implied by the action word (DUCK→head, JUMP→feet, SWORD→torso)?
Are hero and hazard on separate layers (background / hazard / hero) without overlapping MISSING boxes?

auto_probes.name: auto_cutscene_state_exposed
auto_probes.expr: (()=>{const s=window.state||window.gameState;if(!s)return true;const c=['scene','room','level','stage'].some(k=>typeof s[k]==='number');if(!c)return true;return ('lives' in s)&&('qte' in s);})()

auto_probes.name: auto_cutscene_restart_zero_index
auto_probes.expr: (()=>{const g=window.game;if(!g||typeof g.reset!=='function')return true;return (async()=>{g.reset();await new Promise(r=>setTimeout(r,80));const s=window.state||window.gameState;const ks=['scene','room','level','stage'].filter(k=>s&&typeof s[k]==='number');if(!s||ks.length===0)return true;return ks.some(k=>s[k]===0);})()})()

auto_probes.name: auto_qte_input_gated_by_window
auto_probes.expr: (()=>{return (async()=>{const s=window.state||window.gameState;if(!s)return true;const q=s.qte;if(!q||typeof q.expectKey!=='string')return true;let closed=(q.windowOpen===false);if(!closed&&typeof s.sceneEnteredAt==='number'&&typeof q.windowStartMs==='number'&&typeof q.durationMs==='number'){const el=performance.now()-s.sceneEnteredAt;closed=!(el>=q.windowStartMs&&el<=q.windowStartMs+q.durationMs);}if(!closed)return true;const succ=v=>/success|hit|correct|pass/i.test(String(v||''));const r0=q.result;window.dispatchEvent(new KeyboardEvent('keydown',{code:q.expectKey,key:q.expectKey,bubbles:true}));await new Promise(r=>setTimeout(r,60));const r1=((window.state||window.gameState).qte||{}).result;return !(!succ(r0)&&succ(r1));})()})()

recipe.fix_hint: If a check fails: replace drawBg()/large fillRect scene painting and MISSING fallback branches with drawImage(bg_scene) using generated background PNG paths, then draw generated sprite overlays. Keep prompts huge and readable. QTE keydown must set inputFlash/lastInputCode immediately and compute openNow from performance.now so input is visible to the harness. Do not regenerate frames to smooth motion; still-frame cadence is intended. If the threat never visibly approaches (static until the key), animate it toward the threatened body part across the cue->hit window with pointOnPath so the timing reads from the picture, not just the word. Hazard alignment: normalized-room-animation component + qte-hazard-body-alignment playbook; expose __harnessThreatSample().

fix_hints.5: Animate threat along hazardPath with pointOnPath/hazardPosAt across cue→hit; see qte-hazard-body-alignment playbook.
fix_hints.6: threatens field must match action: DUCK=head, JUMP=feet, SWORD=body/torso.
fix_hints.7: Layer order: bg drawImage, then hazard sprite, then hero; no pink MISSING.

content: Mechanism: cards/tiles laid out on a tabletop, dragged or clicked between stacks/piles; deck + hand + foundation areas. Solitaire, freecell, memory, card-battler, mahjong family.

checklist: Are card faces visible with rank or suit symbols?
Are cards arranged in piles or rows on a table?
Is a deck or draw pile visible?
Is a score or move counter visible?
recipe.fix_hint: Cards look blank/static: draw each card face with its rank+suit (text or symbol) on a rounded-rect, render piles as overlapping offsets, and on pointerdown raise the dragged card and follow the cursor; never just fill a plain rectangle.

content: Mechanism: aim-and-launch a projectile under gravity at targets/structures; trajectory arc, collisions, knock-down. Angry Birds, cannon, basketball-toss, artillery family.

checklist: Is a launcher and projectile visible before firing?
Are targets or structures visible to hit?
Is a trajectory or aim indicator visible?
Is a score or shots-left HUD visible?

auto_probes.name: auto_projectile_state
auto_probes.expr: (()=>{const s=window.state||window.gameState;if(!s)return true;const p=s.projectile||s.ball||s.ammo;if(!p)return true;return typeof p.vy==='number'||typeof p.vx==='number'||typeof p.x==='number';})()

recipe.fix_hint: Projectile flies straight or doesn't fall: integrate gravity each frame (vy += g*dt; y += vy*dt), launch with vx/vy from aim angle+power, and resolve collisions with targets; show a faint predicted arc while aiming.

content: Mechanism: dark top-down dungeon where the player carries light; torches/lamps cast radial glow, the rest is shadow (fog of darkness). Roguelike-lighting, horror-explorer family.

checklist: Is most of the screen dark with a lit area around the player?
Does the lit region look like a soft glow (not full bright)?
Are torches or lamps visible as light sources?
Is the dungeon readable inside the lit area?
recipe.fix_hint: Whole screen is bright or light is a hard circle: composite a dark overlay each frame and punch radial-gradient holes (globalCompositeOperation) at the player and each torch; the gradient must fade to transparent at its edge for a soft falloff.

content: Mechanism: touch-first layout with on-screen controls — virtual joystick / d-pad and tap buttons — sized for a phone/tablet portrait or landscape screen.

checklist: Are on-screen touch controls visible?
Are controls placed at screen edges for thumbs?
Is the game content visible above the controls?
Do controls look large enough to tap?
recipe.fix_hint: No visible touch controls: draw a virtual joystick (base+thumb circle) bottom-left and action button(s) bottom-right, wire pointerdown/move/up on the canvas, and keep them as a semi-opaque overlay sized for thumbs.

content: Mechanism: enemies/creeps travel a fixed path while the player places towers/turrets that auto-target and fire; waves escalate. Covers tower-defense / creep-routing games.

checklist: Is an enemy path or route visible?
Are towers or turrets visible on the field?
Are enemies visible on or near the path?
Is a gold, lives, or wave HUD visible?
recipe.fix_hint: If a check fails: drive enemies along a normalized path with pointOnPath(t); draw the path first, then towers, then enemies, then projectiles + HUD. Towers auto-acquire the nearest in-range enemy each tick and spawn a visible projectile/beam. Expose window.state (enemies[], towers[], money, lives) and window.game.reset.

content: Mechanism: notes scroll toward a fixed hit line; the player presses the matching lane key when a note overlaps the line; timing accuracy scores. Covers rhythm / music-timing games.

checklist: Is a hit line or target zone visible?
Are notes or markers visible on screen?
Are input lanes clearly distinguished?
Is a score or combo HUD visible?
recipe.fix_hint: If a check fails: scroll notes from a timeline using performance.now; judge a hit when |noteTime-now| <= window on the correct lane key. Draw the hit line, approaching notes, then hit/miss flashes + score/combo HUD. Set state.lastInputCode/inputFlash on keydown; expose window.state and window.game.reset.

content: Mechanism: a primary resource grows from clicks and from purchased generators that produce passive income over time; upgrades raise rates. Covers idle / incremental / clicker games.

checklist: Is the main currency counter visible and large?
Is a clickable main button or object visible?
Is a list of upgrades with costs visible?
Are unaffordable upgrades visibly disabled or greyed?
recipe.fix_hint: If a check fails: track state.resource as a number; add per-tick passive income from owned generators in update(dt) with real dt; clicks add a flat amount. Render the big counter, the click target, and an upgrade list whose costs grey out when unaffordable. Expose window.state and window.game.reset.

content: Mechanism: the player dodges dense, patterned streams of enemy projectiles in an arena; a small hitbox and many simultaneous bullets define the genre. Covers bullet-hell / danmaku / shmup-boss games.

checklist: Are many enemy bullets visible on screen?
Is the player ship visible and distinct from bullets?
Are bullets distinct from the background?
Is a score or lives HUD visible?
recipe.fix_hint: If a check fails: spawn bullets from emitter patterns into a bullets[] array advanced each frame; draw the player last so it reads above the field. Keep the player hitbox small. Expose window.state (player, bullets[], score, lives) + window.game.reset; set inputFlash/lastInputCode on keydown.

content: Mechanism: words/letters appear and the player must type them, often before they reach a line or expire; correct keystrokes advance, wrong ones miss. Covers typing / word games.

checklist: Are target words or letters clearly visible?
Is typed progress shown on the current word?
Is a score or WPM HUD visible?
Is the text large enough to read?
recipe.fix_hint: If a check fails: track state.target string + state.typed index; on keydown compare e.key to target[typed] and advance or count a miss (text entry uses e.key, not e.code). Render the word with the typed prefix highlighted + score/WPM HUD. Expose window.state and window.game.reset.

auto_probes.name: auto_typing_index_in_range
auto_probes.expr: (()=>{const s=window.state||window.gameState;if(!s)return true;const t=s.target??s.word??s.current;if(typeof t!=='string')return true;const i=s.typed??s.index??s.pos;if(typeof i!=='number')return true;return i>=0&&i<=t.length;})()

content: Mechanism: a gravity-driven ball bounces off bumpers and walls; player-controlled flippers at the bottom keep it in play; ramps/targets score. Covers pinball games.

checklist: Are two flippers visible at the bottom?
Is a ball visible on the table (not stuck in launch lane)?
Are bumpers or targets visible?
Is a score HUD visible?
Are table/ball/bumpers drawn as sprites (not bare circles) when assets were requested?
recipe.fix_hint: Integrate ball with gravity + substeps; on circle hit push out along normal THEN reflect with optional per-id hit cooldown to stop bumper ping-pong. Plunger: charge on Space, release with vy 600-900 and vx into playfield (not pure vertical). Flippers rotate on key; rising flipper adds upward boost. Draw table, bumpers, ball via drawImage when PNGs exist. Expose window.state.

auto_probes.name: auto_launch_increases_speed
auto_probes.expr: (()=>{return (async()=>{const s=window.state||window.gameState;if(!s)return true;const b=s.ball;if(!b)return true;const x0=b.x,y0=b.y,sp0=Math.hypot(b.vx||0,b.vy||0);window.dispatchEvent(new KeyboardEvent('keydown',{code:'Space',bubbles:true}));await new Promise(r=>setTimeout(r,400));window.dispatchEvent(new KeyboardEvent('keyup',{code:'Space',bubbles:true}));await new Promise(r=>setTimeout(r,300));const sp1=Math.hypot(b.vx||0,b.vy||0);const moved=Math.hypot((b.x||0)-x0,(b.y||0)-y0);return sp1>sp0+50||moved>20;})()})()

auto_probes.name: auto_body_enters_playfield
auto_probes.expr: (()=>{return (async()=>{const c=document.querySelector('canvas');const s=window.state||window.gameState;if(!c||!s)return true;const b=s.ball;if(!b||typeof b.y!=='number')return true;if(typeof window.game?.reset==='function'){try{window.game.reset();}catch(e){}}else if(typeof s.reset==='function'){try{s.reset();}catch(e){}}else{window.dispatchEvent(new KeyboardEvent('keydown',{code:'KeyR',key:'r',bubbles:true}));await new Promise(r=>setTimeout(r,80));}window.dispatchEvent(new KeyboardEvent('keydown',{code:'Space',bubbles:true}));await new Promise(r=>setTimeout(r,450));window.dispatchEvent(new KeyboardEvent('keyup',{code:'Space',bubbles:true}));await new Promise(r=>setTimeout(r,700));return b.y<c.height*0.85&&b.x<c.width*0.88;})()})()

content: Mechanism: the player sneaks through a space patrolled by guards with vision cones; entering a cone raises alert/detection; the goal is to reach an objective unseen. Covers stealth games.

checklist: Is the player avatar visible?
Are guards visible with vision cones?
Is a detection or alert indicator visible?
Are guards and player visually distinct?
recipe.fix_hint: If a check fails: draw each guard's vision as a cone/triangle and test point-in-cone for the player each frame to raise state.alert; patrol guards along waypoints. Draw the level, guards + cones, then the player. Expose window.state (player, guards[], alert) and window.game.reset; set inputFlash/lastInputCode on keydown.

content: Mechanism: a procedurally-generated tile dungeon explored on a discrete grid, usually turn-based, with loot and descent through levels and permadeath. Covers roguelike / dungeon-crawl grid games.

checklist: Is a tile-grid dungeon visible?
Is the player avatar visible on a tile?
Are walls distinct from walkable floor?
Is an HP or depth HUD visible?
recipe.fix_hint: If a check fails: build the dungeon with a seeded generator (buildGrid(w,h,seed)), not a giant literal; move the player one cell per keypress and block wall cells. Draw floor, walls, items/enemies, then the player. Expose window.state and window.game.reset.

content: Mechanism: the player drops/places pieces to build a tower as high as possible; physics or alignment decides stability and a misaligned/overbalanced stack topples. Covers stacking / balance-tower games.

checklist: Is a stack or tower of pieces visible?
Is the next piece visible before placement?
Are pieces visually distinct?
Is a height or score HUD visible?
recipe.fix_hint: If a check fails: track stacked pieces in an array with positions; on drop, test alignment/overlap with the piece below and either lock it (raising height/score) or topple it. Draw the stack bottom-up, then the incoming piece + HUD. Expose window.state (stack[], height, score) and window.game.reset.

content: Mechanism: side-view scrolling stage; ONE hero walks left/right; multiple enemies spawn from off-screen and close in; waves or floor boss — NOT a fixed 1v1 duel. Kung-Fu Master / Double Dragon family.

checklist: Is one hero character visible?
Are enemies visible on screen?
Is a health bar or lives HUD visible?
Is a score or wave HUD visible?
recipe.fix_hint: If enemies never appear: spawn from off-screen x beyond camera+width each wave; camera follows player.x. Do NOT use a 1v1 two-fighter state machine — one hero, enemies[] array, facing flips by vx sign only.

content: Mechanism: grid of colored gems/tiles; player swaps adjacent pieces; 3+ in a row/column clear, pieces drop, cascades chain. Bejeweled / Candy Crush family.

checklist: Is a grid of colored gems or tiles visible?
Are gem colors clearly distinct?
Is a score or move counter visible?
Is the board centered and readable?
recipe.fix_hint: Swap must tentatively commit then revert if no 3+ run in row AND column. After clear: gravity drop then refill from top; loop cascades until stable.

content: Mechanism: first-person or perspective vector wireframe rendered with 2D canvas strokes — Battlezone tank combat, Star Wars trench runs. NOT textured raycaster FPS; glowing lines on black.

checklist: Is the background dark with glowing vector lines?
Do lines show perspective depth?
Are entities drawn as wireframe outlines (not filled sprites)?
Is a crosshair or cockpit wireframe visible?

auto_probes.name: auto_wireframe_yaw_or_lateral
auto_probes.expr: (()=>{const s=window.state||window.gameState;if(!s)return true;const p=s.player||s.camera||s.hero;if(!p)return true;const yaw=(p.yaw!==undefined?p.yaw:(p.angle!==undefined?p.angle:p.heading));if(typeof yaw==='number')return true;return typeof p.x==='number';})()

recipe.fix_hint: Wireframe missing: use 2D canvas beginPath/moveTo/lineTo/stroke — NOT three.js or drawImage sprites. Perspective wrong: rotate to camera space then project screenX=cx+(rx/rz)*fov; screenY=cy-(y/rz)*fov; sort by descending rz. Crosshair missing: draw a small + at canvas center each frame.

content: Mechanism: first-person voxel sandbox — place/break cube blocks on chunked terrain with hotbar selection.

checklist: Does the view show 3D voxel blocks with depth?
Are at least two block types visually distinct?
Is a hotbar or block selector visible?
Can the crosshair highlight a targeted block face?

auto_probes.name: auto_voxel_player_has_yaw_or_angle
auto_probes.expr: (()=>{const s=window.state||window.gameState;if(!s)return true;const cam=s.camera||s.player;if(!cam)return true;const yaw=(cam.yaw!==undefined?cam.yaw:(cam.angle!==undefined?cam.angle:cam.heading));if(typeof yaw==='number')return true;return typeof s.yaw==='number';})()

auto_probes.name: auto_voxel_hotbar_has_multiple_types
auto_probes.expr: (()=>{const s=window.state||window.gameState;if(!s)return true;const hb=s.hotbar||s.blockTypes||s.palette||s.blocks;if(!hb)return true;if(!Array.isArray(hb))return true;const ids=hb.map(b=>(b&&typeof b==='object'?(b.id!==undefined?b.id:b.type):b));const uniq=new Set(ids.filter(x=>x!==undefined&&x!==null&&x!==''));return uniq.size>=2;})()

recipe.fix_hint: Mono-color blocks: hotbar must list >=2 distinct block type ids and draw each with its own texture/color. Break/place not working: raycast from camera, not from player feet.

content: Mechanism: player ship fixed in a bottom lane moves left/right and fires upward; enemies enter in formation at the top then dive. Galaga/Centipede/Space Invaders family.

checklist: Is the player ship visible in the bottom zone?
Are enemies visible above the player?
Is the playfield non-blank?
Are entities drawn as sprites (not bare rectangles) when assets were requested?

auto_probes.name: auto_enemies_spawn_ahead_of_player
auto_probes.expr: (()=>{const s=window.state||window.gameState||window.game;if(!s)return true;const p=s.player||s.ship||s.hero;if(!p||typeof p.y!=='number')return true;const es=s.enemies||s.aliens||s.invaders||s.segments||[];if(!Array.isArray(es)||!es.length)return true;const ahead=es.filter(e=>e&&typeof e.y==='number'&&e.y<p.y-8);return ahead.length>0;})()

recipe.fix_hint: Ship moves diagonally: fixed shooter = vx ONLY on left/right keys; player.y stays in bottom lane. Bullets invisible: push to state.bullets[] with vy<0 on fire. Formation drift: store fx,fy slot per enemy; lerp back after dive. Sprites: drawImage ASSETS[name] — not fillRect placeholders.

fix_hints.1: Player not visible: check bottom-lane spawn and drawImage call.
fix_hints.2: No enemies: verify formation/entry spawn loop runs.
fix_hints.3: Blank playfield: draw background and entities before HUD.
